evals.prompt: Use Oz cloud agents to observe replatforming quality for https://talkingslop.ai, https://wordpress.org/news/, and a public Shopify storefront in parallel. Gather the results without modifying replatform-site.
evals.expected_output: One approved computer-use-enabled Oz batch, one propose-only observer result per site, and a validated aggregate cross-site report without shared-skill edits.
assertions: Launches one remote computer-use-capable child per site in one shared batch.
Runs every child in observer propose-only mode.
Keeps inner observer metrics separate from Oz cloud-run metrics.
Does not let parallel children edit replatform-site.

evals.prompt: Run an Oz-orchestrated improve-and-verify pass over four Webflow sites. Use the observer results to make one generalized replatform-site improvement and validate it across all sites.
evals.expected_output: Parallel baseline observations, one centralized DIF applied by the parent, parallel candidate validation using the same agents, and a keep/revert decision.
assertions: Selects a cross-site proposal supported by at least two sites or a blocker.
Applies exactly one centralized DIF rather than concurrent child edits.
Reuses the same child agents for candidate validation.
Keeps or reverts the diff using aggregate quality and cost gates.

evals.prompt: Gather visual replatforming observations for twelve sites using Oz, but keep credit use controlled and report any incomplete cloud runs.
evals.expected_output: A bounded remote execution plan, explicit approval before launching credit-consuming batches, and a partial-safe aggregate report.
assertions: Does not silently exceed the default maximum parallel batch size.
States the number of cloud runs and possible validation phase before launch.
Handles blocked, failed, and malformed results explicitly.
Reports telemetry coverage without estimating missing credits or tokens.

evals.prompt: I've never used Oz before. Run an Oz-orchestrated observation of https://talkingslop.ai and make sure the cloud agent can build and preview the generated Vercel-ready site.
evals.expected_output: A discovered or automatically created reusable web-build environment, verified tooling, and an observation batch pinned to that environment.
assertions: Discovers an existing suitable default environment before creating one.
Creates a personal, repo-less, glibc-compatible Node environment when none exists.
Uses a lightweight smoke run to verify Node, npm, Vercel CLI, and preview tooling before the browser batch.
Uses the resolved environment ID for the cloud-agent batch.
